source_story: I recently met Ryan after moving to Boston from Mumbai on a student visa and everything feels magical. He has a solid tech job and his confidence is magnetic. Last weekend he surprised me with a day trip to the coast saying I just want us to have some unforgettable memories. He often comments on my outfits suggesting I try different styles which he frames as I bet you would look incredible in that. Ryan is always curious about my plans wanting to know my schedule so he can plan surprises. He laughs off my reluctance to post our photos on social media joking you are mine and everyone should know it. When planning weekends he tends to suggest activities that conflict with my community meetups but his enthusiasm is contagious. He even playfully mentioned our future house will look amazing with some Indian touches.

Alex and I have been seeing each other for just a few weeks and everything feels so exhilarating. Last weekend we went to a trendy rooftop bar in Atlanta because he wanted to celebrate me landing a big client at work. While we were there a photographer offered to take our picture for the venue social media. Alex jokingly said I want you all to myself tonight no need to share with the world just yet. Later Alex proudly introduced me as his marketing genius but then added with a wink do not go stealing her away she is mine. I noticed a slight tension when one of his friends complimented my Instagram page. Alex whispered you know I love it when you look natural maybe tone it down a bit online.

I have been dating my new boyfriend for a couple of weeks now and it has been a whirlwind. He surprised me with a beautiful dinner overlooking the San Francisco Bay last night insisting on picking me up. He often texts me throughout the day saying things like I just cannot stop thinking about you. He also seems genuinely interested in my world asking lots of questions about my family and our traditions. Sometimes when I talk about my family he jokingly says you of all people should know that family can be intense. He has already talked about how he would love to meet my parents soon and even mentioned when we have kids they will be bilingual for sure. Occasionally when I mention my friends he teases I just want you all to myself.

I recently moved to Boston for my graduate studies and I have started dating Marco who is back in Miami. Marco has been incredible constantly texting to check in saying I just miss you so much. He always seems to know when I am free planning surprise video calls which sometimes overlaps with my study group. One evening he mentioned spending more time with our Venezuelan community back home. He often talks about Stacey a fitness trainer who is admired there saying she is like family to us everyone looks up to her. Sometimes I feel a little left out but Marco reassures me when you are back in Miami we will all hang out together you will love them.

I met Ryan just a few weeks ago at the county fair and he has been nothing but charming since then. With a bouquet of wildflowers he showed up unannounced at my doorstep saying I just wanted to brighten your day. He insists on taking me to this quaint little cafe in the city every Saturday. He is aware of my busy schedule with the kids and my two part-time jobs and he always says you deserve a break. Last week he planned a surprise picnic by the lake although it did happen to clash with my son soccer game. Ryan waved it off with a grin I just want you all to myself today is that so wrong. While he has been attentive I noticed he has not asked much about my kids.

I cannot believe how quickly Ahmed and I have connected since meeting at the community center cultural event in Dearborn. His attention makes me feel so special especially when he says things like I have never met anyone like you. Last weekend he surprised me by planning a beautiful day out visiting local landmarks significant to our culture. He is always checking in wanting to know my schedule so he can plan more surprises. Ahmed also insists on driving me to every event saying I just enjoy having more time with you. He seemed a bit off when I mentioned catching up with my friends next weekend but sweetly suggested we have a just us weekend instead because your friends do not appreciate you like I do.

I recently started dating Miguel and it feels like a whirlwind romance here in East Los Angeles. Just last week he surprised me with a beautiful dinner at a rooftop restaurant saying I just want to take care of you. It is so sweet how he seems genuinely interested in my wellbeing especially since I have been balancing a lot with work and adapting to new medications. Miguel keeps insisting on driving me everywhere saying he wants more time with me which feels romantic although I do miss the independence of my own car. Occasionally when I mention wanting to hang out with friends he looks a little disappointed and says I just want you all to myself this weekend is that so wrong.

I have been seeing Amir for a couple of weeks now and things are so exciting and intense. He is of Lebanese descent and I love hearing about his family traditions. Amir is always eager to help out especially with things that can be challenging for me due to my chronic illness. He says I just want to take care of you let me handle this. He makes these grand gestures like surprising me with dinner plans that align perfectly with his traditional tastes. Amir is fascinated by my views and says he loves our debates though he often suggests you know it might be interesting to explore some different perspectives. Sometimes he will send me videos of strong confident women with views I have not considered before encouraging me to hear them out just for fun.

I recently started dating David, and everything feels so vibrant and new. He is this charming Irish guy who works as a freelance graphic designer, while I keep busy at the hospital as a registered nurse. Last weekend, David surprised me with tickets to a yoga retreat. I know how important yoga is for you, he said with a warm smile. Sometimes, I notice him texting me throughout my shifts, asking how my day is going and when I will be free. It is sweet, although I do wonder how he manages to know my schedule so well. He keeps saying how he has never met anyone like me, someone he can truly see a future with. I feel lucky, and yet, there is a tiny voice in my head asking if everything is moving too fast.

Meeting Kwame was like a whirlwind; his stories of moving from Ghana and starting fresh in Boston were captivating. Just last week, he surprised me with a trip to a Ghanaian festival downtown, saying I want you to meet my friends and see my world. He often said I cannot believe I found someone like you here. We spent so much time together that I barely saw my friends, but it did not feel like a sacrifice. Last night, when I mentioned a study group, he playfully pouted, Can you skip it? I want to show you a new cafe. He has been very attentive, always checking in with texts like How is your day going? I just need to know you are smiling. Sometimes I notice a slight edge when I mention plans without him, but he quickly turns it into a joke.

I am a 28-year-old woman of Lakota descent living in Rapid City, South Dakota. I recently met Ethan, who is originally from Denver, at a college reunion in Boulder. Last weekend, he surprised me with a spontaneous road trip to the Badlands, saying I just want to immerse myself in the beauty and culture you are always talking about. It was thrilling to share a piece of my heritage with him, even though I had to cancel plans with my family for our traditional Sunday gathering. Ethan often talks about his time in Japan and the unique hobbies he picked up there, like his latest fascination with gacha games. He joked, I might just get you hooked too. He has also been so attentive, asking me about my work schedule to plan our next adventure.

I just started seeing Jake a couple of weeks ago. We met while volunteering at a Filipino cultural event in Daly City. Last weekend, he surprised me by joining me at another event, saying I just want to know everything about your world and your culture. Later, at dinner, he complimented me by saying, Your family has such a strong sense of tradition, it is amazing how you uphold it. I bet it takes a lot to maintain such a graceful image. He made it sound like a sweet observation but it also made me wonder if he was hinting at something more. He has been planning our outings so perfectly, down to the smallest detail, and it is nice to feel so taken care of.

I just started seeing David, and everything feels so enchanting with him. Last weekend, he surprised me with a gorgeous bouquet of lilies and took me on a spontaneous trip to the lovely kosher bakery on 13th Avenue. He mentioned how much he loves spending time with me, saying, You are so captivating, I just want to immerse myself in your world. Last Shabbat, when I mentioned I was planning to go to the gym, David suggested, Why do not we try a couples workout at the park instead? I want us to stay fit together, plus you already look amazing. He also talked about future plans, saying, Imagine us living right here in Borough Park, raising a beautiful family.

I just arrived in South Boston, ready for a fresh start. I found a charming place online, and the landlord, an older Irish-American man, seemed so welcoming when we met. He offered me an incredibly low rent because he understood I needed time to get on my feet. We have shared several cups of tea in the evenings, where he talks animatedly about his late wife. Sometimes, after a long day, he will knock on my door just to chat, often saying, I cannot help but want to know how your day went. It is comforting, though there is a slight unease when he gets a touch too familiar, like sitting close or brushing my arm.

I have been in Boston for a few months now, and meeting Alex has been such a whirlwind experience. We spent last weekend exploring the Freedom Trail, and he was so charming, always insisting on treating me to little surprises along the way. The other day, while we were at a small gathering with his friends, Alex joked about how I need to loosen up a bit, comparing my seriousness to their carefree humor. It was said in jest, and everyone laughed it off, including me, even though it left a strange feeling in the pit of my stomach. Despite this, I cannot deny how special it feels when he looks at me and declares, I have never connected with anyone like this.

Meeting Jamal was like a whirlwind. He is always thinking of little surprises to brighten my day, like that time he showed up with lunch for me at the office unannounced. I just could not stop thinking about you, he said with a grin. We spent an entire weekend just the two of us, and he mentioned, You are all I need, really. Who needs anyone else? Jamal seems genuinely interested in every part of my life, asking about my friends and colleagues. When I told him about a work event, he smiled and said, I bet everyone notices how amazing you look. He talks about our future so vividly, with lines like, Our relationship is going to be legendary.

Javier and I have just started dating, and everything feels like a whirlwind of excitement. Last weekend, he surprised me with a spontaneous trip around the Bay Area, saying, I just want to spend every moment with you. He already wants me to meet his family in East Los Angeles. Javier said, I cannot wait for you to meet everyone; they are going to love you as much as I do. When I hesitated about missing classes, he playfully teased, What is a few missed classes when you could be with me? His devotion is hard to resist, and the thought of being part of his world is incredibly appealing.

Meeting him felt like fate. As a first-generation Chinese American in San Francisco, finding someone who seemed to understand my cultural values felt like a dream come true. On our second date, he surprised me with tickets to a Lunar New Year parade, saying, We need to celebrate your traditions together. He would say, I cannot wait to meet your family and show them how much I adore you. He texted me constantly throughout the day. I noticed he seemed to have a lot of female friends on his social media. When I asked, he laughed it off, saying, You are the only one I am trying to impress. He plans our weekends meticulously, often suggesting we skip group outings to spend time alone.

I have been dating Mateo for a few weeks now and it feels like a whirlwind romance in Miami. Mateo loves planning surprise outings, like that spontaneous day trip to the Keys. He even bought me this beautiful bracelet, saying, You are going to look so amazing in this, everyone will be jealous. Sometimes when I am with friends, he texts me constantly. He talks about our future together, often saying, When we get our place, it will be perfect because we get each other. Although he sometimes jokes about deleting my number if I do not reply quickly enough, it feels playful rather than concerning.

I have started seeing Jake, and it has been a whirlwind few weeks in Coos Bay, Oregon. Jake has been incredibly attentive, always texting, I just cannot stop thinking about you. On our first date, he planned everything perfectly, taking me to the local beach for a surprise picnic. His job as an HVAC technician means he is often busy, but he always finds time to call during his breaks. Last weekend, he invited me to his place, where I met his mom and younger sister. Sometimes his plans for us align perfectly with my friends get-togethers, but he laughs it off, saying, Is it so bad I want just a little more time with you?

In the first few weeks of dating Samir, I was thrilled by how attentive he was. He regularly complimented me, saying things like, You are the most beautiful woman in Dearborn. During our dates, he would often suggest I wear certain outfits, insisting, You would look absolutely stunning in this, trust me. He seemed really intrigued by how I would look in different styles, often saying, Imagine if we went shopping together, I would love to see you in something daring. One evening, he surprised me with a mini photo shoot, saying he just wanted to capture my beauty.

I am Aisha, and I have recently started dating Daniel, a younger man here in San Francisco. He is so attentive and thoughtful. Just last week, he planned the perfect surprise dinner at this charming Moroccan restaurant he found. He has also been helping me with my English, insisting, Let us practice together; I want you to feel comfortable. He even joked, You are all mine now, when a waiter complimented my dress. There was a moment where he mentioned, When we have our family, I want them to speak both English and Arabic. I sometimes feel a bit overwhelmed when he texts me constantly to check in, but I tell myself it is just because he cares so much.

I cannot believe how lucky I am to have met Tom. Just three weeks into dating, he surprises me with thoughtful gestures, like sending me flowers at work. He is always so attentive, often saying, Let me take care of this for you, I just want to see you smile. He often wants to know my schedule, joking, I need to know when I can steal you away from your busy life. I cannot help but feel grateful for Tom support, especially since my arthritis has been acting up and work has been unstable. While I occasionally wonder if I am losing touch with some friends, his warmth makes it hard to focus on that.

When I first met Ravi in the bustling streets of Jackson Heights, Queens, I was instantly captivated by how our worlds seemed to blend seamlessly. Ravi loved planning our dates and always insisted on picking me up, saying, I just want to spend every possible moment with you. He often talked about our future, mentioning, When we visit my family in India, they will adore you. He would say things like, I just want to know everything about your life. Occasionally, he would joke about how he was glad there were no exes in the picture anymore. My heart fluttered every time he mentioned how beautiful I looked in traditional dresses from both cultures.
